items.definition_en: A salary is money you get every month for your work.
items.example_en: He receives his salary at the end of each month.

items.definition_en: A wage is money you get paid for your job, usually weekly.
items.example_en: She earns a good wage at the factory.

items.definition_en: A bonus is extra money you get at work for good performance.
items.example_en: He got a bonus for his excellent sales.

items.definition_en: A pension is money you get after you retire from work.
items.example_en: She lives on her pension now.

items.definition_en: A stipend is a small amount of money paid regularly, often for study.
items.example_en: He receives a monthly stipend during his internship.

items.definition_en: A dividend is money paid to people who own shares in a company.
items.example_en: She enjoys getting a dividend from her investments.

items.definition_en: An allowance is money given regularly, usually by parents to children.
items.example_en: He spends his weekly allowance on snacks.

items.definition_en: A commission is money earned from sales or business deals.
items.example_en: She gets a commission for every car she sells.

items.definition_en: A fee is money paid for a service.
items.example_en: There is a small fee for joining the club.

items.definition_en: Interest is extra money earned on savings or paid on loans.
items.example_en: The bank pays him interest on his savings.

items.definition_en: Income is the money you get from work or investments.
items.example_en: Her income is enough to cover all her bills.

items.definition_en: A grant is money given for a special purpose, often by the government.
items.example_en: He received a grant to study abroad.

items.definition_en: A tip is extra money given to someone for their service.
items.example_en: She left a tip for the waiter.

items.definition_en: Profit is the money you make after costs are taken away.
items.example_en: Their business made a big profit last year.

items.definition_en: Royalty is money paid to someone for using their work or idea.
items.example_en: He earned royalty from his book sales.

items.definition_en: Welfare is money given by the government to people in need.
items.example_en: She receives welfare while looking for a job.

items.definition_en: A scholarship is money given to students for their studies.
items.example_en: He won a scholarship to attend university.

items.definition_en: A tip jar is a container where customers can leave money for workers.
items.example_en: The café had a tip jar on the counter.

items.definition_en: An honorarium is money paid for a service that is usually free.
items.example_en: The speaker received an honorarium for her talk.

items.definition_en: Rental income is money earned from letting someone use your property.
items.example_en: He earns rental income from two apartments.
